title: IMGUI Manual Layout
use_for: designing explicit Rect-based layout for IMGUI framework hosts and panels
replacing GUILayout as the main composition mechanism
controlling geometry deterministically for windows, overlays, toolbars, and content panes
do_not_use_for: architectures that accept GUILayout as the main layout engine
rules: keep ownership of Rect allocation at host and container boundaries
treat GUILayout as an exception for trivial leaf convenience only
make split, dock-like, and overlay geometry deterministic and reviewable
procedure: Define the major host regions that require explicit geometry ownership.
Compute Rect allocation rules for those regions from retained layout state.
Pass resolved Rects into container and leaf draw boundaries.
Keep geometry decisions separate from feature logic and style logic.
output: deterministic geometry model for IMGUI framework composition

primary_when: the request is specifically about manual Rect-based layout, custom geometry allocation, or avoiding GUILayout in IMGUI framework design
must_not_own_when: the request is mainly about overall IMGUI architecture or input routing

solves: how to own framework geometry directly instead of delegating it to GUILayout
how to keep panel placement and split behavior deterministic and reviewable

core_principles: Host-level structure should be expressed as explicit geometry.
Framework layout must be predictable before any leaf widget is drawn.
Geometry ownership belongs to the framework, not to automatic layout helpers.
constraints: Major layout regions must not depend on GUILayout state.
Split and overlay calculations must be stable across IMGUI passes.
Geometry helpers must remain transparent enough to review quickly.
decision_rules: Use manual Rect computation for host shells, sidebars, content panes, overlays, and reusable panel containers.
Allow GUILayout only when a tiny leaf section would not affect host geometry ownership.
Reject abstractions that hide where panel bounds come from.

preferred_patterns: container APIs that receive bounds explicitly
layout state records that drive deterministic geometry
avoid_patterns: geometry inferred implicitly from nested layout groups
feature modules choosing their own host-level panel bounds

recipes.name: Build a split-pane host without GUILayout
recipes.when: Use when a tool window needs stable sidebar and content geometry.
steps: Store split ratio or pixel width in retained layout state.
Compute sidebar and content Rects at the host level.
Pass those Rects into the corresponding panel draw functions.
Keep resizing behavior explicit and separate from leaf rendering.

pitfalls: Letting convenience layout helpers slowly reclaim ownership of host geometry.
Mixing style concerns into geometry computation.
Hiding split logic in nested helpers until panel bounds are no longer obvious.
review_checklist: Are major panel bounds computed manually?
Can reviewers see where each Rect comes from?
Has GUILayout been kept out of host-level composition?
Is geometry stable across layout and repaint?

references.title: Local Reference: IMGUI Composition
references.interpretation: Use this first for the domain's explicit preference for manual host geometry and slot-based composition.
references.why: Use for the domain-wide position that major framework geometry should be manual and host-owned.

references.title: Unity Manual: IMGUI Layout Modes
references.interpretation: Use this to understand what Unity provides, not as a reason to make GUILayout the architectural default.
references.why: Use as the baseline description of what Unity offers, while keeping manual layout as the preferred framework design choice.
